CEN Response Viewer

Response 219987201

Living Innovation Lab

Community Engagement (CE) - source indicates reciprocal or jointly beneficial collaboration.

Activity Basics

Question

Activity Description (Short)

In FY26, the partnership between Arizona State University and GoDaddy made substantial progress in establishing a first-of-its-kind model focused on student-athlete entrepreneurship, digital brand development, and innovation. Through GoDaddy’s Empower initiative and ASU’s SPORTx and J. Orin Edson...

Question

Activity Start Date / End Date

Start Date

2025-07-01 (inferred from FY26 survey window)

End Date

2026-06-30 (inferred from FY26 survey window)

Question

Public Service or Community Engagement

Community Engagement (CE) - source indicates reciprocal or jointly beneficial collaboration.

People And Units

Question

ASU Faculty/Staff Involved

  • Jennie Denison (Director Global Corporate Advancement) - jennie.denison@asufoundation.org
  • Additional ASU collaborators from survey: This partnership has been deeply collaborative across multiple ASU and ASU Foundation teams, reflecting the cross-functional nature and strategic scale of the initiative. Key collaborators include SPORTx at ASU and the J. Orin Edson Entrepreneurship + Innovation Institute, which have led program development, athlete engagement, entrepreneurship programming, and national expansion strategy. The ASU Office of the COO has played an important leadership and alignment role in advancing the partnership vision and institutional coordination. Additional collaboration has included ASUF Writing Services for proposal and communications support
  • ASUF General Counsel and ASU General Counsel for legal review, intellectual property, NIL, and compliance considerations
  • ASUF Gift Agreements for structuring and execution of philanthropic support
  • Outreach Hub for communications and visibility strategy
  • and ASUF Donor Relations for stewardship, reporting, and donor engagement. Together, these teams have helped position the partnership as a scalable, enterprise-level model for student entrepreneurship, athlete innovation, and university-industry collaboration.

Question

ASU Units Involved

  • GCA (survey abbreviation
  • full ASU unit name not specified in source)
  • ASU Foundation
  • Innovation Institute
  • The ASU Office
